title: Japan eliminated from World Cup after comeback loss to Brazil
summary: The Japanese team lost to Brazil and was eliminated from the tournament, sparking reactions from players, fans, and media worldwide.

title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, declaring Trump's order unconstitutional
summary: The highest US court affirmed that children of undocumented immigrants born in the country are citizens, rejecting President Trump's attempt to eliminate this right.

title: Japanese government approves reform of Imperial Household Law to allow adoption of males from former imperial families
summary: The amendment seeks to secure succession to the throne through the adoption of male descendants, sparking intense political debate and criticism from the opposition.

title: Yen plunges to historic lows, surpassing 162 yen per dollar
summary: The Japanese currency reached levels not seen since 1986, raising concerns about the impact on the economy and citizens' lives, as authorities evaluate measures.

title: Japan's Lower House approves law punishing desecration of national flag
summary: The bill, promoted by the ruling coalition and supported by Nippon Ishin, was approved with the absence of the entire opposition, which considers it a threat to freedom of expression.

title: Ayano Sato takes office as new Bank of Japan board member without defining her stance on interest rates
summary: The new board member, appointed by the Ishiba government, stated that core inflation is not as strong as thought, suggesting she will not support an imminent rate hike.

title: US Supreme Court allows restricting transgender athletes' participation in female sports
summary: The court endorsed regulations that limit the eligibility of transgender athletes in female categories, a ruling affecting the LGBTQ+ community.

title: Trump claims negotiations with Iran are taking place in Doha, but Tehran and Qatar deny it
summary: The US president stated that talks with Iran were being held in Qatar, but both the Iranian government and Qatari authorities denied this version.

title: Japan Airlines to return 300 million yen for improperly receiving state aid
summary: The Japanese airline admitted to submitting incorrect applications to obtain subsidies for the development of flying vehicles, after being detected in an internal audit.

title: Japan Post to sell children's books at its branches to alleviate bookstore shortage
summary: The pilot initiative at four offices on the Fukushima coast will offer a hundred children's literature titles, in response to bookstore closures in the region.

title: Chinese Communist Party reaches 112.8 million members on its 105th anniversary
summary: Membership grew by more than one million in the last year, and a third of current members joined during Xi Jinping's era, according to official data.

title: Suspect arrested for homicide in Hiroshima linked to gambling debts
summary: A man was arrested for murder during a robbery in Mihara city; security cameras captured two individuals heading to the crime scene.

title: Mitsuru Morishita hits walk-off home run to give Tigers victory in the tenth inning
summary: Morishita's decisive hit allowed Hanshin to come back against the Yomiuri Giants, in a game that kept excitement until the end.

title: Ishiba government presents first economic policy framework without mentioning fiscal consolidation
summary: The basic budget document prioritizes expansive growth and removes reference to fiscal consolidation, which could increase public debt.

title: Global stock markets close higher with Nikkei gaining more than 500 points
summary: Optimism driven by artificial intelligence and defense demand lifted indices in Japan and Europe, with the Stoxx 600 reaching a record.

title: Baseball day: Nippon Ham wins, SoftBank rises to lead
summary: Pitcher Kitayama achieved his eighth consecutive victory, while SoftBank surpassed Seibu and took the top of the standings.

title: Advances in artificial intelligence in Japan: from medical training to typhoon prediction
summary: Various projects show the potential of AI in diagnostics, weather forecasting, and the rise of open source, although ethical challenges remain.

title: New initiatives to boost female participation in politics and employment in Japan
summary: Tokyo's first labor law for women's promotion comes into effect, while a group of lawmakers seeks to pave the way for a female prime minister.

title: Debate in Japan over consumption tax and minimum wage
summary: While the opposition proposes a consumption tax reduction, the minimum wage target promised by former Prime Minister Ishiba is postponed.

title: New activities in Antarctica: naming rights at Syowa Station and major exhibition
summary: To fund research, sponsorship rights at the Antarctic station are offered, while an interactive exhibition opens in Tokyo for the 70th anniversary of exploration.
